definition:
1. to split  or cleave off , (Odyssey by Homer) 
2. to sever  or detach from , τινὰ ἀπό τινος (Herdotus Historicus):—;Pass., ἀποσχισθῆναί τινος to be separated from  . . ., (Herdotus Historicus); of a river, to be parted from  the main stream, (Herdotus Historicus); of a tribe, to be detached from  its parent stock, (Herdotus Historicus) 
3. metaphorically, ἀπ. τινὰ τοῦ λόγου to cut  him off  from his speech, interrupt  him in  it, (Aristophanes Comicus) (ML)
